title: Can delayed home construction cost you Section 54 relief? ITAT answers
summary: Sold a house and reinvested the gains but missed the construction deadline due to Covid? The Chennai ITAT says that alone is not enough to deny Section 54 tax exemption. The Tribunal held that pandemic-related delays cannot be treated as negligence and allowed the capital gains exemption claim.

title: Paying over ₹50,000 in monthly rent? Here's why you must deduct TDS before making payment to your landlord
summary: Individuals paying monthly rent above ₹50,000 are required to deduct TDS at 2% before making the payment to the landlord. Timely deduction, deposit of TDS through Form 26QC and issuance of Form 16C can help avoid unnecessary penalties and tax notices.

title: What health insurance retention data reveals—and conceals
summary: Mint's analysis of insurers' NL-47 disclosures shows the average retention rate for top-selling retail health insurance plans was just 73% in FY26. What's driving the churn?&nbsp;

title: ITR filing AY 2026-27: 5 key tax rules that intraday and F&O traders must know before filing returns
summary: ITR filing for AY 2026-27 requires intraday and F&amp;O traders to report business income correctly using ITR-3. Check the latest disclosure rules, business codes, turnover reporting requirements, tax audit thresholds, filing deadline and common mistakes to avoid for smooth tax compliance.

title: 8th Pay Commission: Can salaries rise by over 60%? Here's how HRA, TA & DA proposals may boost pay for level 1 payments
summary: The 8th Pay Commission could significantly increase central government salaries if proposals on fitment factor, HRA, TA and DA merger are accepted. While estimates suggest a 60% rise in some cases, the government is yet to take a final decision.

title: ITR filings move at a slow pace as deadline nears: Expert explains why an extension beyond 31 July is unlikely this year
summary: With the July 31 deadline approaching, only a small percentage of taxpayers have filed their returns. Cleartax CEO Archit Gupta warns against assuming an extension this year, as slower filing rates could lead to increased pressure before the due date.

title: Income-Tax Returns: What is authorised representative? Check full list of circumstances, services, eligibility here
summary: Income-tax returns: Today we explain the circumstances under which taxpayers can appoint authorised representative, the requirements, eligibility criteria and other important factors.

title: ITR 2026: Senior citizens can claim ₹50,000 deduction even without health insurance —Here's how
summary: The income tax return filing deadline is approaching, prompting taxpayers to gather documents. Section 80D allows senior citizens without health insurance to claim up to ₹50,000 for medical expenses, available only under the old tax regime.

title: Bought US startup shares using overseas savings? Here's how India will tax it
summary: A returning NRI who invested overseas savings in a US startup may not face tax on the purchase itself, but discounted acquisitions and annual foreign asset disclosures can trigger tax and compliance obligations.

title: EPFO: Taxation rule on Voluntary Provident Fund — the ₹2.5 lakh VPF threshold, explained
summary: VPF allows employees to contribute more than the mandatory EPF rate for a larger retirement corpus. Contributions over ₹2.5 lakh face taxation on excess interest. VPF matches EPF's interest rate and qualifies for tax deductions under Section 80C, with specific withdrawal conditions.
